description: Fix two medium-severity bugs in the MCP server: (1) FSWatcher instances missing 'error' event handlers that cause process crashes on Linux inotify exhaustion, and (2) WebSocket URL scheme mismatch in the site analyzer that silently prevents frame samples from being attached to WebSocket endpoints.

userStories.title: Add error event handlers to all FSWatcher instances in file-watcher.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want FSWatcher error events to be handled gracefully so that inotify resource exhaustion on Linux does not crash the MCP server process.
acceptanceCriteria: All FSWatcher instances created by watchPlugin(), watchPendingPlugin(), and startConfigWatching() have an 'error' event handler attached immediately after creation
The error handler logs a warning with the plugin name (or 'config') and the error message, then closes the watcher
An FSWatcher error event does NOT cause an uncaught exception or process crash
Unit test in file-watcher.test.ts verifies that emitting an 'error' event on an FSWatcher does not throw and produces a log warning
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Fix WebSocket URL scheme mismatch in detect-apis.ts frame sample matching
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using plugin_analyze_site, I want WebSocket frame samples to be correctly attached to their endpoints so that the analysis output includes actual frame payload examples for WebSocket APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: A helper function normalizes WebSocket URLs by converting wss:// to https:// and ws:// to http:// for consistent origin comparison
The normalization is applied both when creating WebSocket endpoint URLs (lines 416-423) and when matching frame URLs (lines 449-456)
detectApis() correctly attaches frame samples to WebSocket endpoints when the endpoint URL uses https:// but frame URLs use wss://
detectApis() correctly attaches frame samples to WebSocket endpoints when both use the same scheme
Existing unit tests in detect-apis.test.ts continue to pass
New unit test verifies frame sample attachment with wss:// frame URLs matched against https:// endpoint URLs
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
